data.title: Bitdeer files $1B mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• Bitdeer Technologies (NASDAQ:BTDR) filed a prospectus related to a mixed shelf offering to raise up to $1B.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.

data.title: Star Group announces definitive acquisition agreement
data.description:
• Star Group (NYSE:SGU) on Thursday said it has entered into a definitive agreement to purchase a home energy distributor, located within the company’s existing operating footprint, for approximately $68 million before working capital adjustments.
  • Star anticipates closing on the transaction within the next 45 days. Additional terms were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: micromobility.com appoints Gian Luca Spriano as CEO
data.description:
• micromobility.com on Thursday said it has appointed Gian Luca Spriano as chief executive officer (CEO) and member of the board of directors, effective December 9, 2024.
  • Spriano succeeds Salvatore Palella, who has stepped down from his roles as CEO and director. Palella, the company’s controlling shareholder, has no remaining executive or board position within the organization.
  • Spriano has been with the company since July 2018, serving in key roles including Head of Business Development and most recently as chief financial officer (CFO) since December 2023.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rio Tinto to invest $2.5B to expand Rincon lithium project in Argentina
data.description:
• Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) has announced plans to invest $2.5B to expand its Rincon lithium project in Argentina.
  • The investment will boost the project’s production capacity to 60,000 tonnes of battery grade lithium carbonate per year, comprised of the 3,000-tonne starter plant and 57,000-tonne expansion plant.
  • Construction of the expanded plant is scheduled to begin in mid-2025, with first production expected in 2028 followed by a three-year ramp up to full capacity.
  • Located in the heart of the ‘lithium triangle’ in Argentina, Rincon’s mine life is expected to be 40 years.
  • RIO shares were down 1.57% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Initial jobless claims unexpectedly jump in past week
data.description: Initial jobless claims for the week ended Dec. 7 rose by 17K to 242K, well above the 221K consensus and 225K prior (revised from 224K), the U.S. Department of Labor said on Thursday.

data.title: Stock index futures fall sharply on hotter than expected wholesale inflation report
data.description:
Stock index futures traded in the red on Thursday as November producer price index came in hotter than expected.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.31%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.18%.

November Producer Price Index, or PPI came in at +0.4% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus, and +3.0% Y/Y vs. +2.6%. Core PPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus, and +3.4% Y/Y vs. +3.2% consensus.

Initial jobless claims unexpectedly jumped in the past week.

U.S. stocks finished mostly higher in Wednesday's history-making session, as an in-line consumer inflation report bolstered expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week. Nasdaq had ended above 20K, with the Big Tech boosting the index.

"Markets put in a decent performance yesterday, as the U.S. CPI report came in broadly as expected, which was seen as giving the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to cut rates next week," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"When it comes to the Fed, the CPI print left little doubt among investors that another rate cut will happen next week," Allen added.

Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 10.1% in premarket trade after the Photoshop creator issued guidance for fiscal 2025 that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.

More on markets

  • Behind the monster rally that drove the Nasdaq to 20,000
  • CPI Report: Inflation Is No Longer A Concern
  • Reaction Roundup: Experts weigh in on November consumer inflation report
  • The Recipe For A Bull Market

data.title: ECB cuts interest rate by 25bps as expected, fourth cut this year
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) slashed its interest rate by 25 basis points to 3.00% on Thursday as broadly priced by the market. This is the fourth cut this year.

The decision is based on its updated assessment of the inflation outlook and the strength of monetary policy transmission.

The bank outlined that its staff sees its headline inflation averaging 2.4% in 2024, 2.1% in 2025, 1.9% in 2026, and 2.1% in 2027. "Most measures of underlying inflation suggest that inflation will settle at around the Governing Council’s 2% medium-term target on a sustained basis," the bank said in a statement.

It now expects slower economic recovery than September projections.

The interest rates on the deposit facility, the main refinancing operations, and the marginal lending facility will be decreased to 3.00%, 3.15%, and 3.40%, respectively, with effect from 18 December 2024.

ETFs to track: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

data.title: Nano Labs reports ~360 bitcoin holdings as of December 12
data.description:
• China-based circuit design company Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) held ~360 bitcoins as of December 12, with a current value of about $36.22M.
  • The average acquisition cost of its bitcoin holdings comes to roughly $99.7K per bitcoin, inclusive of transaction fees.
  • NA shares were trading +4.25% Thursday pre-market at $8.09.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SHARC International Systems names Michael Albertson as CEO
data.description:
• SHARC International Systems (OTCQB:INTWF) said it has appointed Michael Albertson as its new CEO, president and director.
  • Albertson will succeed Lynn Mueller, who has been the firm's CEO, president and chairman since 2014.
  • Mueller will stay on as executive chairman.

data.title: 180 Life Sciences regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• 180 Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATNF) said it has regained compliance with Nasdaq's listing rule.
  • The firm was non-compliance with Nasdaq's rule of maintaining an audit committee consisting of three independent directors.
